search_queries_used: site:bsaci.org rhinitis guideline mouth breathing nasal obstruction snoring examination
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic adenoid hypertrophy, rhinitis, septal deviation, or sleep-apnoea diagnosis
automatic allergy test, sleep study, steroid, antihistamine, surgery, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Mouth breathing has nasal, adenoidal, structural, sleep, oral, behavioural, and other causes.
No investigation, medicine, procedure, treatment, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children or adults with mouth breathing in a possible rhinitis context; sleep-disordered breathing, craniofacial, paediatric adenoid, and structural disease require additional evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, paediatric, allergy, dental, sleep, or ENT assessment.
UAE_applicability: BSACI evidence requires UAE paediatric, allergy, sleep, dental, prescribing, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official BSACI 2017 exact history, examination, and investigation sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2017 revised edition remains listed by BSACI; material recency uncertainty remains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact rhinitis history section directly supports onset, nasal obstruction, mouth breathing, sleep or snoring impact, associated nasal, eye or lower-airway features, relevant negatives, and contextual triggers without diagnosing rhinitis or adenoid disease.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact examination section supports clinician observation of mouth breathing and general, nasal, oral, throat, septal, secretion, crusting, or polyp findings without auto-populating them.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation context supports review of already available specialist information and a clinician-entered plan or follow-up without recommending testing or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, duration, daytime or sleep pattern, obstruction, snoring, apnoea, allergy, adenoid, septum, dental and speech impact, examination, testing, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
